title: Jeremy Pleasant
content:
People.ContactEmail jpleasant@mccarthy.ca
- Dalhousie University
Jeremy Pleasant is a Partner in our Business Law Group in Toronto and is a member of our National Private Equity Practice. Jeremy acts for various public and private companies, including many leading financial sponsors, on a wide range of domestic and cross-border M&A and corporate finance transactions, particularly in the technology, energy transition, infrastructure and real estate industries.
Jeremy was previously based in the Firm’s New York office, where he focused on Canada/U.S. cross-border M&A transactions and financings, providing Canadian corporate advice to U.S. based clients acquiring assets or conducting business in Canada.
Some of Jeremy’s experience includes acting for the following clients:

#### Mergers and Acquisitions
- Summit Industrial REIT in connection with its C$5.9 billion acquisition by a joint venture between GIC and Dream Industrial REIT
- Brookfield Infrastructure and Evoque Data Center Solutions, in its acquisition of Cyxtera Technologies
- CVC DIF (formerly DIF Capital Partners) in connection with its acquisition of a majority stake in Diverso Energy, a leading developer, owner, and operator of geothermal energy systems in Canada.
- Slate Asset Management L.P. in connection with its US$2.33 billion portfolio and platform acquisition from Annaly Capital Management
- Power Sustainable Lios in connection with its acquisition of Crofter’s Organic and its investment in Goodleaf Farms
- ACR Group in its acquisition of APS Aerospace Corporation
- The management shareholders of 9 Story Media Group in connection with Scholastic's US$186 million investment in 9 Story Media Group
- Investment Management Corporation of Ontario in connection with its co-investment with Torquest Partners into VersaCold Logistics Services
- Mill Road Capital Management LLC in connection with the sale of PRT Growing Services Ltd.
- Slate Asset Management L.P. in connection with its sale of a minority non-voting interest to Goldman Sachs Asset Management’s Petershill Program
- Fairfax Africa Holdings Corporation in connection with its strategic transaction with Helios Holdings Limited
- Griffon Corporation in connection with its acquisition of ClosetMaid Corporation from Emerson Electric Co.
- Slate Asset Management L.P. in its going-private acquisition of Huntingdon Capital Corp.
- Jamieson Wellness in connection with its $300 million initial public offering and the $273 million secondary bought deal offering of its common shares by CCMP Capital Advisors, LP.
- Fairfax Africa Holdings Corporation in connection with its initial public offering and certain of its African investments.
- Stelco Holdings Inc. in connection with its $230 million initial public offering.
- Farmers Edge Inc. in connection with its $125 million initial public offering.
- Slate Asset Management L.P. in connection with the formation and financing of multiple private equity funds, including Slate European Real Estate Limited Partnership I, Slate European Real Estate Limited Partnership II and Slate Canadian Real Estate Opportunity Fund I L.P
- Slate Office REIT in connection with its $191 offering of subscription receipts to finance its acquisition of seven office properties; its $120 million offering of subscription receipts to finance its acquisition of three office properties and its $80 million offering of subscription receipts to finance its acquisition of a portfolio commercial properties from Fortis Properties Corporation.
- The Empire Life Insurance Company in connection with its initial public offering of $149.5 million of rate reset preferred shares.
Jeremy completed a secondment with Slate Asset Management where he advised on various matters including securities, corporate governance and M&A.
Jeremy frequently speaks on M&A, corporate governance and related topics, including co-teaching the advanced securities law program at Western University. Jeremy received his J.D. from the Schulich School of Law at Dalhousie University in 2012 and a B. Comm in Finance from Saint Mary’s University in 2009. While at the Schulich School of Law, Jeremy was on the Dean’s List, received numerous academic awards and prizes, including the Blakes Scholar Award, and served as the Vice President of the Dalhousie Black Law Students’ Association.
Jeremy serves as a member of the firm’s Ontario Student Committee.
- The Canadian Legal Lexpert Directory Leading Lawyer: Corporate Mid-Market; Mergers & Acquisitions; Private Equity
